label: Past Promotion
title: A.I.-Optimized SDLC Assessment
subtitle: Turn scattered A.I. usage into a governed software delivery process.
summary: A focused assessment and design sprint for software teams that want to use A.I. across the SDLC without losing control of quality, security, or accountability.
description: I evaluate the current delivery process, identify safe A.I. opportunities, define practical guardrails, and provide a short roadmap the team can actually use.
The goal is faster delivery with stronger process, review, and confidence.

attributes.title: 12-hour sprint
attributes.body: Current-state review, opportunity map, guardrails, and roadmap.

attributes.title: Practical outputs
attributes.body: Use-case map, verification gates, and accountability model.

attributes.title: Best fit
attributes.body: Small to mid-sized software teams using or evaluating A.I. tools.

attributes.title: Goal
attributes.body: Faster delivery with stronger process, review, and confidence.

sections.title: What this covers
sections.body: A current-state review of how the team delivers software, where A.I. is already appearing, and where it can safely help.

sections.title: What you receive
sections.body: A practical opportunity map, recommended guardrails, verification checkpoints, and a short roadmap for responsible adoption.

label: Featured Promotion
title: What Can A.I. Do for You?
subtitle: A friendly, practical A.I. session for everyday people who want useful help without the jargon.
summary: A plain-language A.I. discovery session for people who are curious about A.I. but do not know where to start.
description: I help you find simple ways A.I. can support daily tasks, communication, reminders, photo sharing, planning, writing, organizing, and staying connected with the people who matter.
This is for people who want useful guidance without jargon, coding, or technical pressure.

attributes.title: 60-minute session
attributes.body: Friendly walkthrough, real-life examples, and simple next steps.

attributes.title: Practical uses
attributes.body: Messages, reminders, photos, planning, writing, organizing, and everyday tasks.

attributes.title: Best fit
attributes.body: Consumers, families, caregivers, retirees, and curious non-technical users.

attributes.title: Goal
attributes.body: Leave with a short personal A.I. plan you can actually use.

sections.title: Bring your real life
sections.body: The session starts with the tasks, questions, and frustrations you already have. Then we identify where A.I. can help simply and safely.

sections.title: Inspired by the Series campaign
sections.body: This offer continues the same idea: making A.I. make sense for real people by focusing on connection, confidence, and practical everyday usefulness.

label: Past Promotion
title: Industry A.I. Fusion Assessment
subtitle: Find practical, low-disruption ways to apply A.I. inside a real business operation.
summary: A practical A.I. assessment for business owners and operators who want to identify useful A.I. opportunities without chasing hype or disrupting the way their teams already work.
description: I review the way the business currently serves customers, manages work, handles staffing pressure, tracks information, and responds to operational friction.
The result is a short, practical opportunity map showing where A.I. can help first, what should wait, and how to adopt it with the least amount of disruption.

attributes.title: Business-use review
attributes.body: A focused review of customer service, staffing, operations, workflows, and recurring friction.

attributes.title: Opportunity map
attributes.body: Clear recommendations for where A.I. can help now, where it may help later, and what to avoid.

attributes.title: Best fit
attributes.body: Food service, healthcare, hospitality, manufacturing, retail, and service-based businesses.

attributes.title: Goal
attributes.body: Identify practical A.I. use cases that improve responsiveness, accuracy, efficiency, and customer experience.

sections.title: What this covers
sections.body: The assessment looks for practical A.I. opportunities such as customer service automation, predictive staffing, inventory support, scheduling, maintenance alerts, personalization, operational reporting, and workflow simplification.

sections.title: Inspired by the Industry A.I. series
sections.body: This promotion grew out of the industry-focused A.I. education series covering food service, healthcare, hospitality, manufacturing, and retail.
